steps.title: Identify the assumption to challenge
steps.content: The assumption is explicit: snakes do not detect sound traveling through the air. To challenge it directly, a finding must show snakes CAN detect and respond to airborne sound.

steps.title: Spot the finding that proves detection
steps.content:
Choice D shows all three snake species displaying more than 20 cm of movement in response to airborne sound—matching their response to ground sound.

This directly proves the assumption false. The other three choices show either weak or absent airborne detection (A, C) or make a hearing comparison unrelated to detection itself (B).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
